title: Return Values Practice
instructions: Practice returning single and multiple values from functions.
hints: Return multiple values as comma-separated: return val1, val2
Use tuple unpacking to get multiple return values

testCases.description: calculate_rectangle should return correct area and perimeter
validate_age should return True and message for valid age
testCases.validation:
is_valid, msg = validate_age(25)
assert is_valid == True and msg == 'Valid age', 'Age 25 should be valid'

is_valid, msg = validate_age(-5)
assert is_valid == False and msg == 'Age cannot be negative', 'Negative age should be invalid'

testCases.description: parse_email should split valid email into username and domain
testCases.validation: assert parse_email('user@example.com') == ('user', 'example.com'), 'Should parse email into username and domain'

testCases.description: parse_email should return (None, None) for invalid email
testCases.validation: assert parse_email('invalid-email') == (None, None), 'Should return (None, None) for email without @'

testCases.description: get_stats should return correct min, max, and average

description: Learn to return single and multiple values from Python functions using tuples. Implement functions that return area and perimeter calculations, validation results with messages, parsed email components, and statistical summaries demonstrating effective use of tuple return values.
